results.rendered_html:
nāgaraka (wie eben)
1〉 adj.
a〉 in der Stadt wohnend, subst. Städter im Gegens. zu āraṇyaka  Z. d. d. m. G. 7,310, N. 1 .
— b〉 = nāgara 1,b; von Planeten  VARĀH. BṚH. S. 33,22 .
— c〉 geschickt, gewandt (in gutem und schlechten Sinne, kutsanaprāvīṇyayoḥ)  P. 4,2,128 . caura, citralekhaka Schol. Daher bei  WILS.  m. Dieb; Maler.
— 2〉 m. Stadthaupt, Polizeimeister  ŚĀK. 73,1 . v. l. für nāgarika.
— 3〉 wohl n. getrockneter Ingwer (vgl. nāgara)  SUŚR. 1,161,9 .

results.rendered_html:
nāgaraka, nāgarika a. [nagare bhavaḥ vuñ]
1 Town-bred, town-born.
2 Polite, courteous, courtly; nāgarikavṛttyā saṃjñāpayaināṃ  Ś. 5 ; sādhu ārya nāgarikosi  V. 2. 
3 Clever, shrewd, cunning (vidagdha).
— kaḥ
1 A citizen.
2 A polite or courteous man, a gallant; one who shows exaggerated attention to his first mistress while he is courting some one else.
3 One who has contracted the vices of a town.
4 A thief.
5 An artist.
6 The chief of the police;  V. 5 ;  Ś. 6. 
— kaṃ Dry ginger.
— (rikaṃ) The toll levied from a town.
